mapped_rules.notes: Match parameters schema and GameConfig types reflect canonical match parameters.
Turn phases implemented in state machine and applyAction; architecture doc references RULES.md for phases.
Combat resolution and damage application are implemented in resolveAttack.
Suit behaviors (e.g., boundaries, damage behavior) are encoded in combat logic; explicit mapping is present in RULES.md but not repeated in separate docs.

mapped_rules.rule_section: Pass logic and Special Start (RULES.md §§7, pass rules)
mapped_rules.notes: Pass and SpecialStart behavior controlled in validateAction/applyAction; tests cover pass rules.

rules_without_engine_mapping: Some advanced narrative examples and DSL aspects in RULES.md are not explicitly mapped in any separate documentation asset (mapping is implicit via engine code and tests).
engine_logic_without_rule_reference: Engine support for dynamic grid sizes (engine/tests/dynamic-grid.test.ts) is documented in architecture/plans but not explicitly cross-referenced from RULES.md beyond global constraints.
Bot AI behavior (engine/src/bot.ts) intentionally out-of-scope from RULES.md but is core to some tests.

event_docs: docs/RULES.md (event emission model, span-based event description, replay guarantees)
docs/system/ARCHITECTURE.md (transaction log and hashing model, phaseTrace, hash semantics)
missing_event_topics: A single consolidated 'Event Model' document aligning RULES.md span-based model with transaction log implementation.
Explicit event versioning strategy beyond specVersion.
event_model_verification.replay_explanation_quality: Strong but split across RULES.md and ARCHITECTURE.md; together they form a coherent replay story, but there is room for a single canonical event model reference.

missing_topics: Explicit scaling/HA strategy for MatchManager and WebSocket connections.
Runbook-level diagrams for deployment topology and dependencies.
Step-by-step guide for modifying rules and updating corresponding tests/fixtures.
Explicit doc on how to add golden replay fixtures and run drift checks beyond rules:check.

gameplay_documents: docs/system/SITE_FLOW.md (site/player flow for the web experience)
missing_explanations: More visual or example-driven explanation of phalanx formation and board layout for non-technical players.
A condensed 'Quick Rules' guide or player handbook separate from the full canonical spec.

documentation_strengths: Single canonical rules source at docs/RULES.md, explicitly called out as authoritative in README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md.
Architecture documentation (docs/system/ARCHITECTURE.md) tightly couples with the rules spec by referencing RULES.md for turn phases and explaining hashing/transaction log.
Strong terminology discipline with canonical vocabulary defined in RULES.md and reused in architecture/dev docs.
Developer workflow and validation commands are clearly documented in README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md, including rules:check and schema:check.
Rich set of engineering plans and design docs in docs/plans/ that record design intent and future roadmap.
critical_gaps: No single, consolidated 'Event Model' document that unifies the span-based event description in RULES.md with the transaction log/hashing model in ARCHITECTURE.md.
No explicit 'how to change rules safely' contributor flow mapping rules → engine → tests → docs.
Gameplay documentation is heavily oriented toward canonical spec; a shorter player-centric overview could improve accessibility.
Scaling/HA and production runbook documentation is not present as a first-class doc (current material focuses more on architecture and risk than operations).
recommended_improvements: Create a dedicated 'Event Model and Replay' document that references RULES.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and the transaction log schema, clarifying which events are canonical vs telemetry.
Add a contributor-focused 'Rules Change Process' doc that walks through updating RULES.md, engine modules, tests, and any derived docs.
Introduce a concise player-facing 'Quick Rules' or 'How to Play' guide derived from RULES.md but optimized for readability.
Add an operations/runbook doc describing deployment, scaling assumptions, replay verification, and incident handling for match disputes.
Extend developer docs with a short 'Documentation as Specification' section explaining how RULES.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and tests work together to prevent drift.
